title: Senior Manager Product Management
description_excerpt: OverviewAt Zebra, we are a community of innovators who come together to create new ways of working. United by curiosity and a culture of caring, we develop smart solutions that anticipate our customer’s and partner’s needs and solve their challenges.Being part of Zebra Nation means you are seen, heard, valued, and respected. Drawing from our unique perspectives, we collaborate to deliver on our purpose. Here you are part of a team pushing boundaries today to redefine the work of tomorrow for organizations, their employees, and those they serve.You’ll have opportunities to learn and lead in a forward-thinking environment, defining your path to a fulfilling career while channeling your skills toward causes you care about—locally and globally.Come make an impact every day at Zebra.What We're Looking ForThe Product Management team drives the development of product definition and planning to optimize the company's products and solutions. The team applies broad knowledge of company's product and solutions capabilities as well as the competitive environment to identify new growth areas, new technology and emerging product opportunities. Oversees the analysis and evaluation of key market statistics and customer requirements to define product strategies and identify product opportunities to expand market share.The Product Management team also evaluates in-depth market analysis and leads business case development for optimization of existing products and solutions alternatives and/or to create the next generation of new products. Collaborates with research and development to create products and validate early product definitions. This role is both strategic and technical in nature and requires meaningful industry experience.As a Senior Manager, Product Management, you will be responsible for a specific product line contribution to the overall business unit. This includes increasing the profitability of existing products to developing new products for the company. You will build products from existing ideas, and help to develop new ideas based on your industry experience and your contact with customers and prospects.The position has responsibility for the growth and business success of an assigned product line life cycle and/or product category. The role is responsible for managing other product managers in both developing strategies and managing tactical execution of product growth and development plans as well as delivering management objectives, coaching, and mentoringThis position is responsible for ensuring the ongoing review of the competitive environment for his/her product category. This is accomplished through analysis of competitive equipment and compilation of other pertinent findings, and the information thus gained is published and/or presented on a regular basisThe Senior Manager, Product Management ensures that his/her organization works with the Product Marketing and Marketing Communications in developing product promotion programs. title: Senior Product Manager - Hybrid Control Plane for Observability & Management
description_excerpt: We’re not just building better tech. We’re rewriting how data moves and what the world can do with it. With Confluent, data doesn’t sit still. Our platform puts information in motion, streaming in near real-time so companies can react faster, build smarter, and deliver experiences as dynamic as the world around them.It takes a certain kind of person to join this team. Those who ask hard questions, give honest feedback, and show up for each other. No egos, no solo acts. Just smart, curious humans pushing toward something bigger, together.One Confluent. One Team. One Data Streaming Platform.About The RoleConfluent's enterprise customers operate complex, distributed streaming platforms across cloud, on-prem, and hybrid environments. They need world-class observability, management, and governance interfaces to operate these platforms confidently at scale. Unified Stream Manager (USM) and Control Center are the products that deliver this experience — providing operators with the tools to monitor, manage, and govern their Confluent Platform and Confluent Private Cloud deployments.This PM will own the product strategy and execution for our hybrid control plane, ensuring that Confluent delivers an industry-leading operations experience for self-managed and private cloud customers.What You Will DoThe PM will: Define and execute the product strategy and roadmap, driving adoption and customer success Lead the evolution of USM as the modern, cloud-native management and observability surface for Confluent Platform and Confluent Private Cloud Drive the Control Center roadmap, balancing maintenance of the existing product with the strategic migration path toward USM Own the observability story for self-managed Confluent — including metrics integration with third-party monitoring systems (e.g., Dynatrace, Datadog, Prometheus) Collaborate closely with engineering, design, field, and support teams to deliver capabilities that reduce operational burden for customers Measure and report on product adoption, customer satisfaction, and business impact Partner with GTM teams to enable field communication, customer onboarding, and competitive positioningWhat You Will BringThe PM must: Have deep experience with enterprise title: Senior Product Manager - RPay
description_excerpt: Teamwork makes the stream work. Roku is changing how the world watches TVRoku is the #1 TV streaming platform in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, and we've set our sights on powering every television in the world. Roku pioneered streaming to the TV. Our mission is to be the TV streaming platform that connects the entire TV ecosystem. We connect consumers to the content they love, enable content publishers to build and monetize large audiences, and provide advertisers unique capabilities to engage consumers.From your first day at Roku, you'll make a valuable - and valued - contribution. We're a fast-growing public company where no one is a bystander. We offer you the opportunity to delight millions of TV streamers around the world while gaining meaningful experience across a variety of disciplines.  About the Team & Role The Roku Pay, Core Payments team has played a pivotal role in driving Roku’s rapid growth in online subscriptions, powered by a seamless in-streaming, on-TV purchase experience. Supporting a wide range of Roku teams, Core Payments enables transactions across multiple consumer touchpoints — including TV, web, mobile, and advertising. These teams employ diverse strategies to sell digital content, from Subscription Video on Demand (SVOD) and Transactional Video on Demand (TVOD) to Roku-branded devices and shoppable ads directly on the device.Core Payments is now seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead the creation of our Payment Machine Learning Platform — a high-impact initiative designed to optimize payment retries, routing, and authorization success rates for our subscription business. This platform will harness AI/ML to make real-time, data-driven payment decisions at scale, directly influencing revenue growth, reducing churn, and enhancing customer satisfaction. In this role, you will operate at the intersection of machine learning, payments infrastructure, and subscription growth, collaborating with engineering, data science, finance, and payment operations to deliver a platform that is both technically sophisticated and mission-critical. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced, highly technical environment, possesses deep knowledge of payments and subscription economics, and can balance strategic vision with hands-on execution. You will own the roadmap, drive flawless delivery, and continuously improve the platform through experimentation and data-driven insights. What You’ll Be Doing Define and Drive the Roadmap: Partner with engineering, ML, and business stakeholders to define a high-impact roadmap for the Payment ML Platform, aligned to Roku’s subscription growth goals.Translate Business Goals into Product Requirements/Feature Guides, Convert business KPIs (e.g., authorization rate uplift, churn reduction) into clear product requirements, success metrics, and technical specifications. Lead AI/ML Product Development: Collaborate with ML engineers and data scientists to design, train, and deploy models for retry prediction,

title: Sr Product Manager - Marketplace Item setup
description_excerpt: About us:As a Fortune 50 company with more than 400,000 team members worldwide, Target is an iconic brand and one of America's leading retailers. Joining Target means promoting a culture of mutual care and respect and striving to make the most meaningful and positive impact. Becoming a Target team member means joining a community that values different voices and lifts each other up. Here, we believe your unique perspective isimportant, and you'll build relationships by being authentic and respectful.Team overview:Right along with Target’s distinctive retail brand, we are constantly improving and innovating our digital shopping experience. Are you ready to excel in the fast-changing retail environment? Are you a customer service enthusiast who’s curious about the guest and can see the shopping journey through their eyes? Then you’ll have success on the Digital Strategy & Platform team, whose goal is to offer guests a seamless online shopping experience across our platforms—including Target.com and the store network’s fulfillment capabilities. Here, you’ll collaborate to create and implement the digital strategies that enrich the online experience through the right mix of personalization, simplicity and intuitiveness. Your ideas will support Target in complementing the beloved in-store shopping interaction with a convenient, reliable and instantly gratifying digital experience.Role overview:We are seeking a Sr Product Manager - T+ (Marketplace) Item Charter to define and execute the strategy for how Target+ engages, guides, and enables sellers at scale. You will lead the strategy, vision, and execution of our Seller Portal. You will shape the ecosystem that empowers sellers, expands guest choice, and delivers scalable growthIn this role, you will lead a charter that improves seller activation, compliance, performance, and adoption across the marketplace lifecycle. You will own the systems, signals, and experiences that shape seller behavior—driving measurable improvements in marketplace quality, operational efficiency, and seller success.This is a highly cross-functional, data-driven role requiring strong product judgment, systems thinking, and the ability to influence outcomes without authority. You will partner closely with engineering, design, operations, and analytics teams to deliver intelligent, scalable solutions that help sellers take the right actions at the right time.As a Sr Product Manager, you will take lead on - Owning seller lifecycle engagement, including activation, adoption, performance improvement, and re-engagement. Designing data-driven nudges, reminders, and interventions that influence seller behavior and improve compliance, quality, and productivity. Enabling sellers to easily understand what actions they need to take and why, reducing friction and support dependency. Driving measurable behavior change in seller success. title: Senior Product Manager (Payments Platform)
description_excerpt: As a Senior Product Manager specializing in Payments, you will drive the vision, strategy, and execution of innovative payment solutions that enable seamless, secure, and scalable transactions for our customers. You will collaborate with cross-functional teamsincluding engineering, marketing, sales, finance, legal and go-to-marketto deliver global products that delight users, ensure regulatory compliance, and support business growth. This is a highly visible role requiring global payments expertise, technical fluency, and a passion for customer-centric innovation and growth.In this role, you will:Own and articulate the product vision and roadmap for Cvent payment products, ensuring alignment with company goals, evolving market trends, and regulatory requirements.Identify opportunities to expand our payment capabilities across the Cvent ecosystem through new products and/or partnershipsLead the end-to-end product lifecycle: ideation, problem definition, opportunity sizing, prioritization, development, launch, and ongoing iteration.Represent the voice of the customer by gathering feedback and translating customer needs and insights into actionable product opportunities.Define and analyse key metrics to measure customer and product success, leveraging data-driven decision making to inform strategy and prioritisation.Collaborate closely with tech and non-tech stakeholders to deliver high-quality, global payment solutions on time.Stay up-to-date on industry and technology trends, regulatory changes, and emerging payments technologies to inform product direction.Leverage AI to identify and solve customer problems, enhance product capabilities and improve decision-making processes.Identify key opportunities to use AI to externalise event payment capabilities and support AI-initiated workflows.Evangelise the payments products internally and externally, representing Cvent at customer meetings, industry events, and online communities.Required QualificationsBachelors degree or equivalent practical experience; advanced degree (MBA, MS) is a plus.12-16 years of total experience with at least 8-10+ years of product management experience, with a proven track record of delivering successful payment products at scale.Deep understanding of the payments ecosystem, including roles involved, payment processors, global payment methods, networks, risk/fraud management, and regulatory compliance.Experience leading cross-functional teams in a fast-paced, agile environment.Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills; comfortable working with large data sets and analytics tools.Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including experience presenting to executive leadership.Technical background or experience working closely with engineering teams is highly desirable.Demonstrated ability to manage competing priorities and deliver results in a matrixed environment.Passion for building products that solve real customer problems and drive measurable

title: Senior Product Manager
description_excerpt: Senior Product Manager – Platform Efficiency & COGSAbout The RoleAs a Senior Product Manager for Platform Efficiency, you will drive continual “cost to value” alignment by delivering the product features and platform enhancements that ensure durable efficiency of scale. You will sit at the intersection of Product, Engineering, FinOps, and Pricing - responsible for the architectural efficiency and gross margin health of our global SaaS analytics platform.Sumo Logic processes hundreds of terabytes of data daily. Your mission is to ensure that as we scale, our infrastructure costs stay lean and our platform remains a performant, competitive, and high-margin engine. You will own the strategy for optimizing data ingestion, storage, networking, and compute costs while simultaneously improving the platform’s performance and reliability.Key Responsibilities COGS Strategy & Roadmap: Own the end-to-end roadmap for platform efficiency. Identify, prioritize, and execute initiatives that reduce cost-to-serve without compromising customer experience or SLAs. Own product strategy to focus on customer margins across multiple Sumologic revenue products Infrastructure Optimization: Collaborate with Core Platform and Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) teams to optimize resource allocation across Kubernetes, Kafka, and AWS services (S3, DynamoDB, EC2). Cost Observability: Partner with FinOps to ensure internal "Cost Observability" dashboards inform continual efficiency improvements. Use data to identify "hot spots" in our AWS/Cloud infrastructure and drive targeted architectural shifts.Product Improvement Lifecycle: Translate complex technical bottlenecks into actionable PRDs. Define "Definition of Done" for efficiency projects, focusing on metrics like Cost per GB Ingested and Cost of Query engine, CPU utilisation of engineering assemblies. Cross-Functional Leadership: Coordinate across Finance, UX, and Engineering stakeholders to deliver cost optimized solutions that meet the needs of customers as well as the financial goals of the business. Clearly articulate the financial impacts of technical debt and infrastructure modernization to executive stakeholders. Vendor & Cloud Management: Work with cloud providers and 3rd party vendors to optimize licensing, reserved instances, and usage-based billing models.What We’re Looking For Technical Depth: You have a solid understanding of cloud-native architectures, distributed systems, and microservices. Experience with AWS cost structures and FinOps principles is a major plus. Data-Driven Mindset: You are a power user of data analytics tools and have a strong hold on cost and numbers. You don’t just report data; you find the "why" behind the numbers to drive strategic pivots. SaaS Savvy: 5+ years of PM experience, ideally in a technical platform role within an Enterprise SaaS environment (Log Analytics, Observability, or Cybersecurity). Execution Focus: Proven track record of taking high-level efficiency goals and breaking

title: Senior Product Manager (Integrations)
description_excerpt: Cvent is a leading meetings, events, and hospitality technology provider with more than 5,000+ employees and 24,000+ customers worldwide, including 60% of the Fortune 500. Founded in 1999, Cvent delivers a comprehensive event marketing and management platform for marketers and event professionals and offers software solutions to hotels, special event venues and destinations to help them grow their group/MICE and corporate travel business. Our technology brings millions of people together at events around the world. In short, we’re transforming the meetings and events industry through innovative technology that powers the human connection. Cvent's strength lies in its people, fostering a culture where everyone is encouraged to think like entrepreneurs, taking risks and making decisions confidently. We value diverse perspectives and celebrate differences, working together with colleagues and clients to build strong connections.AI at Cvent: Leading the FutureAre you ready to shape the future of work at the intersection of human expertise and AI innovation? At Cvent, we’re committed to continuous learning and adaptation—AI isn’t just a tool for us, it’s part of our DNA. We’re looking for candidates who are eager to evolve alongside technology. If you love to experiment boldly, share your discoveries, and help define best practices for AI-augmented work, you’ll thrive here. Our team values professionals who thoughtfully integrate AI into their daily work, delivering exceptional results while relying on the human judgment and creativity that drive real innovation.Throughout our interview process, you’ll have the chance to demonstrate how you use AI to learn, iterate, and amplify your impact. If you’re excited to be part of a team that’s leading the way in AI-powered collaboration, we’d love to meet you.Disclaimer: Beware of Recruitment Scams – Legitimate Cvent recruiting communications will always come from an official ‘name@cvent.com’ email. We never request any payments or ask for sensitive personal or financial information via chat or social media platforms.
